item.title: Love Isn't Just a Feeling, It's a Skill
item.description: I had this pop up in my algorithm and liked what I read. Source: https://www.instagram.com/p/DMW7thkuK7Y/ One point I'd like to make is that mistakes are inevitable, but I'll never excuse a lie. I used to fixate on adultery being the only betrayal (and avoided it as a result), but I've learnt that betrayal comes in multiple forms. (Yes, I grew up.) Anyway, I figured at least one other person may find this useful.
item.dc:creator: unwaver

item.title: Customize the GF. And other things dump.
item.description:
What if she runaway with my refrigerator or dishwasher ?
2. Perfectly timed firework show picture turns into a work of art.
3. The world's oldest underground station, Baker Street, 157 years apart.
4. A pool from the Roman period, Tunisia.
5. Pervision glasses.
6. Hehe nice sign.
7. Crazy tracked stretch limo.
8. Popping bubble wrap.
9. What a relief.
10. A Clock that spell out the time.
11. I inherited this clock from my mother. It’s called a 6-man clock. You're number six.
12. Beauty of timing.
13. A long “Missouri” battleship made from Lego bricks in a toy store in California.
14. How lawn balls are made for the sport of Pétanque.
15. Gyms should have these.
16. At this age !!
17. Slippin It In At The Truck Stop.
18. Hydrodipping a shopping bag.

item.title: Imagine going to Wendy's and Wendy gives you your order.
item.description:
Also here she is as Piccolo wearing Goku's clothes when they both went to get their drivers license
Still one of the sickest burns I've ever heard
Also her Yamcha cosplay is pretty good.
The Master Roshi is good too
https://www.instagram.com/kangacosplay
item.dc:creator: DropDrop

item.title: If only Donnie thought the same way
item.dc:creator: Xalten2

item.title: I didn't know that this song was recorded by Native Americans.
item.description: “Come and Get Your Love” by the band Redbone. Redbone was a Native American and Mexican-American rock band known for blending rock, funk, and indigenous influences in their music. The song, released in 1973, became a major hit and is recognized for its catchy beat and fusion of styles, including Native American cultural references.
item.dc:creator: iceage2012

item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.title: Welp, there goes that..
item.description: Love them or hate them, the last remaining “original” Imgur staff have been supposedly part of another layoff by parent company MediaLab. While it’s clear things have gone down the tubes in recent years, We’re now in uncharted territory with MediaLab fully at the helm to replace everyone. For the better? For worse? Who knows what’s going to happen to this platform in the next few years…
item.dc:creator: WeatherWiz

item.title: Poison Ivy - Cosplay
item.description:
My favorite cosplay of the year so far. Am so happy with how I got the lighting and how perfectly my backyard acted to accentuate everything! Hope you all enjoy!
Also had to compare... I've come a long way since my first Poison Ivy Cosplay 10 years ago (Right)

item.dc:creator: NeonGravestoneLights

item.title: Laughing for a fundraiser
item.description: I wanted to do some good and put some kindness into the world. My husband's mother is down on her luck, and has had some issues with repairing the house she lives in. It's very old, and in dire need of fixing. As it is, my husband has been the only one to work on things because he doesn't ask to be paid. I've thrown together something to help her so I can surprise her and maybe take some of the weight off her shoulders. https://gofund.me/e3ef67e0
item.dc:creator: Hidatsu

item.dc:creator: stonetemplefox05

item.title: Omg :D
item.description:
Sound on! This is baby Cowboy. I am kitten-sitting him for a few days. He was found with his sister in a yard, alone, at just a few days old. The kid who found him didn’t realize we really needed to wait for mom to come back, so he brought them in. His sister didn’t make it. He’s about 5 weeks and refusing to wean. Just hit 1 pound.
Here he is in his little kitten b & b. I have 3 big kitties in the room, so I need to keep him separated.
I put their food by him so maybe he’ll learn how to eat. I told him he needs to eat so he can run into a big kitty and defeat his enemies.
I suppose after a nap. *This was my first time bottlefeeding ever. I thought I had it at a good angle until I saw the video. The second feeding went much better. :)
item.dc:creator: kittycrazy88

item.title: A minor collision — but what affected me most was how quickly people resort to aggression, manipulation, and lies.
item.description: Last weekend, I picked up family from Tomorrowland in Boom, Belgium. After nearly an hour of waiting in traffic, we were finally on our way home. Just 400 meters before the ramp to the highway towards Brussels, my car was hit by a taxi. Everyone in my car felt the impact. We pulled over, got out, and I immediately saw a scratch on my rear bumper and damage to his car. But instead of having a normal conversation, the taxi driver started yelling: “Nothing happened!” and “That damage was already there!” When I suggested we fill out the paperwork, he got even more aggressive. “Then call the police!” he snapped. So I did — because I could feel that reasoning with him wasn’t going to work. While I was on the phone with the emergency line, he repeatedly accused me of being drunk. “You’ve been drinking, I can smell it!” “You're going to have to blow into a breathalyzer!” “You’re under the influence!” He said it again and again, loud and threatening. It was clear he was trying to scare me, make me doubt myself, shift the blame. Even his passengers — complete strangers — jumped in to berate me, yelling that I was blocking the road “for nothing” and that I should be ashamed for holding up their ride home. Still, I kept my calm. I waited for the police to arrive. I filled in the damage report. And yes — we both had to take a breathalyzer test. And yes — we both tested negative. So yes, there’s damage to my car. And yes, the insurance companies will sort it out. But what stays with me is how quickly people are willing to lie, to bully, and to tear someone down — just to avoid responsibility. Let me be clear: shouting louder doesn’t make you right. And honesty, calm, and integrity may not win immediately — but they win in the end. I’m writing this because it affected me deeply. Because I believe we can do better than this. And because I need to get it off my chest.
item.dc:creator: IWantThatUsername
